turnaround_reason: Acadia was crushed by short-seller reports, DOJ/SEC investigations into patient-holding practices at psychiatric facilities, and concerns about admissions integrity. With litigation overhangs partially digested and earnings beating handily for two straight quarters, the operating story may be inflecting.
bullish_points: Two consecutive massive EPS beats (Q4 593% surprise, Q1 40% surprise) suggest operations stabilizing
Price has reclaimed the 200-day MA (+13%) with 38% short interest providing squeeze fuel
Forward P/E of 13.5 is reasonable; analyst mean target $28.46 implies ~21% upside
Real cash-flow generating business ($182M operating cash flow) with secular tailwind in behavioral health demand
EV/EBITDA of 8.3x is depressed vs historical mid-teens multiple
bearish_points: Net loss TTM (-32.8% profit margin) reflects ongoing litigation/settlement costs
$2.69B debt vs $158M cash — debt-to-equity of 124 is heavy
Zero insider open-market buying despite the drawdown — only stock grants
Regulatory/legal overhang on admissions practices not fully resolved; risk of new probes
30-day price change is -13%, suggesting recent momentum stalled
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings — a third consecutive beat would confirm the operational turnaround and force shorts (38% of float) to cover
suggested_trade: Jan 2027 $25 calls or call spread $25/$35 sized small to capture earnings squeeze
key_risk: New regulatory action or whistleblower revelation reigniting the DOJ overhang; admissions volume guidance disappointment on Q2 print
would_skip_if: Q2 print misses or guidance is cut; any fresh DOJ/SEC headline; if 30-day momentum stays negative into earnings
